name: Kenosha Pass, Colorado
description: Renowned for its vast groves of aspen trees turning brilliant gold in the fall.

name: Telluride
description: Tucked deep in the San Juan Mountains, providing some of the earliest and most dramatic fall colors in the country. The juxtaposition of golden aspens against the rugged, sheer peaks makes this area a prime target. The free gondola offers sweeping panoramic views.

name: Maroon Bells
description: Located near Aspen, arguably the most photographed fall foliage destination in North America. The twin peaks over 14,000 feet perfectly reflect into a high alpine lake, framed by brilliant yellow aspen groves.

name: Kebler Pass
description: A high-altitude, unpaved road that cuts through one of the largest continuous aspen groves in the United States. The sheer scale of the golden canopy here is staggering.

name: Columbia River Gorge
description: Offers a completely different foliage experience, blending big-leaf maples and cottonwoods against dramatic basalt cliffs and rushing waterfalls. The lower coastal elevations keep the colors around later into October.

name: California 158 (June Lake Loop)
description: Winds through the Eastern Sierra and offers California's best autumn show. The contrast of brilliant yellow aspens against stark granite peaks and deep blue glacial lakes is exceptional.

name: Mt. Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest
description: Provides a rare phenomenon: the changing of the larches. Larches are deciduous conifers whose needles turn a brilliant, glowing gold before dropping in late autumn.

name: Kancamagus Highway (NH 112)
description: A 34-mile scenic drive cutting directly through the White Mountain National Forest. It is the quintessential New England leaf-peeping route, famous for its dense concentrations of red sugar maples.

name: Stowe
description: Often dubbed the 'Fall Color Capital,' Stowe offers the classic Vermont village experience complete with white-steepled churches surrounded by rolling hills of red, orange, and yellow.

name: Acadia National Park
description: Offers a rare combination of rocky Atlantic coastline and vibrant autumn forests. The coastal climate slightly delays the color change compared to inland Maine.

name: Blue Ridge Parkway
description: Stretches 469 miles along the crest of the Blue Ridge Mountains, providing a massive, continuous corridor of autumn color that slowly cascades southward and downward in elevation.

name: Great Smoky Mountains National Park
description: Features an incredible diversity of over 100 species of native trees, creating a dense patchwork quilt of colors. It is the perfect final destination for a cross-country foliage trip.

name: Shenandoah National Park
description: A long, narrow national park running along the Blue Ridge Mountains in Virginia, famous for Skyline Drive and its sweeping valley views.

name: Ozark National Forest, Arkansas
description: Experience sweeping views of vibrant fall foliage stretching across the rolling mountains of the Ozarks.

name: Porcupine Mountains, Michigan
description: Known as the 'Porkies', this rugged wilderness offers some of the most spectacular fall colors in the Upper Peninsula.

name: Tahquamenon Falls, Michigan
description: Stunning copper-colored waterfalls surrounded by brilliant autumnal hardwood forests.

name: North Shore Scenic Drive, Minnesota
description: Hugging Lake Superior, this drive boasts stunning views of birch and maple trees erupting in yellow and red.

name: Boston Common & Public Garden
description: An iconic urban leaf-peeping destination featuring weeping willows, maples, and historic monuments.

name: Mount Greylock, Massachusetts
description: The highest point in Massachusetts offers incredible panoramic views of foliage across three states.

name: Adirondack Mountains, New York
description: One of the most famous foliage destinations in the US, featuring vibrant sugar maples and serene mountain lakes.

name: Letchworth State Park, New York
description: Known as the 'Grand Canyon of the East', the gorge and waterfalls look spectacular amidst autumn colors.

name: Pocono Mountains, Pennsylvania
description: Historic towns and sweeping valleys covered in a vibrant blanket of red, orange, and gold.

name: Pine Creek Gorge (PA Grand Canyon)
description: A deeply cut gorge offering spectacular views of autumnal forests lining the canyon walls.

name: Delaware Water Gap, New Jersey
description: Breathtaking views of the Delaware River winding through forested mountains bursting with color.

name: Starved Rock State Park, Illinois
description: Famous for its steep sandstone canyons and waterfalls surrounded by brilliant fall foliage.

name: Brown County State Park, Indiana
description: Nicknamed the 'Little Smokies', this park is famous for its rolling hills and sweeping autumn vistas.

name: Hocking Hills State Park, Ohio
description: Deep gorges, recess caves, and waterfalls illuminated by towering yellow and red hardwood trees.

name: Alpine Loop Scenic Byway, Utah
description: A winding mountain road featuring spectacular golden aspens with Mount Timpanogos in the background.

name: Zion National Park, Utah
description: Vibrant cottonwoods and maples contrast stunningly against the towering red sandstone cliffs.

name: Seattle Japanese Garden, Washington
description: A stunning urban oasis showcasing vibrant Japanese maples during the fall season.

name: Portland Japanese Garden, Oregon
description: Famous for its intricately maintained maples that turn a fiery red each autumn.

name: Monte Sano State Park (Huntsville)
description: A high-elevation spot offering beautiful foliage views from mountain overlooks and the Warpath Ridge Trail.

name: Oak Mountain State Park (Pelham)
description: A central Alabama favorite featuring vibrant colors near Peavine Falls and the Peavine Overlook.

name: Oneonta & Blount County
description: Excellent for a scenic driving tour of historic covered bridges (like Horton Mill and Swann) framed by beautiful fall colors.

name: Joe Wheeler State Park
description: Features beautiful, colorful views along the Tennessee River near the dam and cabins.

name: Cullman County
description: Home to the historic Clarkson Covered Bridge and Ave Maria Grotto, which look striking surrounded by seasonal colors.

name: Denali National Park
description: A premier spot where the vast, treeless tundra transforms into a vibrant, low-lying carpet of oranges, reds, yellows, and purples.

name: Hatcher Pass
description: Offers dramatic views of golden fall colors set against jagged granite peaks, highly popular for hiking and photography.

name: Talkeetna
description: A charming gateway town surrounded by birch, cottonwood, and alder that turn brilliant shades of gold—perfect for viewing via flightseeing tours.

name: Eklutna Lake
description: A popular spot near Anchorage for viewing beautiful fall colors reflecting on the bright blue water while kayaking or hiking the nearby trails.

name: Verde Valley (Dead Horse Ranch State Park)
description: An excellent lower-elevation area for seeing bright yellow cottonwoods and other deciduous trees along the riverbanks.

name: White Mountains
description: Known for a classic, expansive display of golden aspens and oaks spread across high-country vistas.

name: Mount Lemmon (Santa Catalina Mountains)
description: A "sky island" near Tucson offering a rare opportunity to see traditional fall colors at higher elevations in Southern Arizona.

name: Aravaipa Canyon
description: A peaceful, scenic desert canyon known for vibrant, colorful riparian trees lining the creek bed.

name: Mount Magazine State Park
description: The state's highest point, offering panoramic, long-range views of the changing foliage from the Signal Hill Trail.

name: Petit Jean State Park
description: Known for dramatic landscapes where the impressive Cedar Falls waterfall is framed by brilliant autumn colors.

name: Devil’s Den State Park (Yellow Rock Trail)
description: Highly recommended for sweeping, unobstructed vistas of the colorful Lee Creek Valley.

name: Queen Wilhelmina State Park (Rich Mountain)
description: Offers expansive, high-altitude views of the changing foliage, complemented by a historic scenic lodge.

name: Lake Ouachita State Park
description: A perfect spot to enjoy reflections of colorful oaks and maples on the calm waters of Arkansas's largest lake.

name: Eureka Springs
description: A picturesque Victorian town surrounded by hills that show early hints of color and provide a charming, immersive autumn atmosphere.

name: Lake Tahoe
description: Offers a serene experience with pockets of bright fall color surrounding the clear, deep blue waters of the lake.

name: Nevada City
description: A charming Northern California town known for its historic gold-rush feel and abundant autumn colors lining the streets.

name: Oak Glen
description: Famous for apple orchards and a classic fall atmosphere, making it a top spot for colors in Southern California.

name: Julian
description: A historic Southern California mountain town well-known for its cozy autumn atmosphere, apple pie, and seasonal foliage displays.

name: Guanella Pass
description: A popular and accessible high-elevation mountain pass near Georgetown that offers vast, vibrant colors above the treeline.

name: Litchfield Hills (Litchfield, Kent, Washington, Cornwall)
description: Widely considered the premier destination for foliage in the state, famous for rolling hills, dense forests, and historic New England charm.

name: Macedonia Brook State Park (Kent)
description: Offers great hiking with vibrant fall views stretching across the Taconic and Catskill Mountains.

name: Mohawk State Forest (Cornwall)
description: Provides excellent high-elevation vantage points for sweeping views of the seasonal colors.

name: Kent Falls State Park
description: A popular stop featuring a beautiful cascading waterfall surrounded by vivid autumn foliage.

name: Connecticut River Valley
description: Known for its scenic beauty, riverside views, and historic towns that look spectacular when the leaves turn.

name: Sleeping Giant State Park
description: An excellent spot for hiking and immersing yourself in a vibrant canopy of autumn leaves in the mid-state area.

name: Brandywine Creek State Park
description: Known for rolling meadows and lush forests separated by classic stone walls, making it a prime spot for viewing dramatic autumn displays.

name: White Clay Creek State Park
description: Offers extensive trails that wind through deep, colorful layers of reds, golds, and oranges.

name: Mt. Cuba Center
description: A botanical garden that provides a stunning, curated fall foliage experience with excellent photography opportunities.

name: Cape Henlopen State Park
description: Offers a unique coastal palette where pine forests and colorful deciduous trees meet sandy dunes and ocean views.

name: Torreya State Park (Bristol)
description: Widely considered the best place in Florida to see traditional fall foliage, featuring high bluffs and rare hardwood trees that turn gold, red, and orange.

name: Edward Ball Wakulla Springs State Park
description: Famous for its massive freshwater spring, where visitors can view fall colors framing the sapphire waters from trails or a riverboat tour.

name: Paynes Prairie Preserve State Park (Gainesville)
description: Offers a mix of trees providing lovely late-season fall colors, often peaking in late November.

name: Brasstown Bald
description: Georgia's highest peak, offering the premier vantage point for sweeping, panoramic views of the changing autumn landscape across multiple states.

name: Black Rock Mountain State Park
description: The state's highest state park, making it one of the very first locations to show vibrant early-season colors.

name: Vogel State Park
description: Features beautiful views of Lake Trahlyta surrounded by fall colors, with excellent vantage points from the Bear Hair Gap Trail.

name: Amicalola Falls State Park
description: Known for its stunning, towering waterfall, with surrounding trails offering a classic, vibrant North Georgia forest experience.

name: Blood Mountain
description: A popular Appalachian Trail destination providing high-elevation views of the surrounding colorful deciduous forest.

name: Kula (Maui)
description: Located in the "upcountry," it has a cooler climate and various flowers and foliage that provide a subtle, autumn-like aesthetic.

name: Holualoa (Big Island)
description: During the autumn coffee harvest season in the Kona Coffee Belt, crimson coffee cherries pop vibrantly against emerald green leaves, creating a unique harvest-time visual.

name: Boise River Greenbelt
description: A convenient 25-mile path offering beautiful, accessible riverfront foliage right in the Treasure Valley.

name: Galena
description: A premier destination known for its historic charm, rolling hills, and panoramic views of the changing countryside from Horseshoe Mound Preserve.

name: Shawnee National Forest
description: A massive Southern Illinois forest featuring 265,000 acres of woodlands, hills, and lakes that create an extensive, dramatic backdrop for autumn colors.

name: Anderson Japanese Gardens (Rockford)
description: These meticulously maintained gardens become incredibly vibrant and photogenic during the fall season.

name: Starved Rock State Park
description: Famous for its canyons and waterfalls, its forested trails offer stunning vistas of fall colors along the Illinois River.

name: Brown County State Park
description: Often called the "Little Smokies," this is Indiana's most iconic leaf-peeping spot, known for expansive, hilly forests and scenic vistas.

name: Bonneyville Mill County Park (Elkhart County)
description: Features vibrant colors surrounding a picturesque, historic gristmill along the Heritage Trail.

name: Holliday Park (Indianapolis)
description: A popular local destination offering a unique blend of nature trails and historic ruins framed by colorful trees.

name: Tom and Jane Dustin Nature Preserve (Fort Wayne)
description: Highly recommended for immersing yourself in autumn hues along its peaceful hiking trails.

name: Pikes Peak State Park (McGregor)
description: Located in the Driftless Region, it offers iconic, elevated views of the Mississippi River framed by vibrant, dense autumn leaves.

name: Effigy Mounds National Monument
description: Provides scenic overlooks of the Mississippi River valley alongside historic Native American mounds and excellent wooded hiking trails.

name: Ledges State Park
description: Famous for its steep slopes and stunning woodland vistas that fill with bright colors, easily accessible from central Iowa.

name: Hitchcock Nature Center (Loess Hills)
description: Features unique, rolling prairie and woodland landscapes that look spectacular in the fall.

name: High Trestle Trail
description: Offers a unique vantage point to see the turning leaves, particularly from the iconic half-mile-long High Trestle Trail Bridge.

name: Overland Park Arboretum & Botanical Gardens
description: Known for an extensive collection of maples that provide a vibrant burst of autumn color.

name: Daniel Boone National Forest
description: Provides endless scenic drives (like the Zilpo Scenic Byway) and hiking through massive stretches of turning oaks, hickories, and maples.

name: Old Frankfort Pike
description: A scenic byway winding through the heart of Bluegrass horse country, offering a quintessential Kentucky experience surrounded by autumn colors.

name: Cumberland Falls State Park
description: The massive "Niagara of the South" waterfall provides a beautiful backdrop to the oaks and maples during the autumn months.

name: Kisatchie National Forest
description: Louisiana's only national forest is a premier spot for leaf-peeping, featuring the Longleaf Trail Byway and a mix of hardwoods that turn brilliant amber, gold, and red.

name: Chemin-A-Haut State Park
description: Situated on a high bluff overlooking bayou land, this park is famous for woodland scenery and ancient cypress trees that take on striking golden hues.

name: Jimmie Davis State Park
description: Features a hilly landscape with a mix of pine and hardwood forests where rustic trails and lake sunsets highlight the late-season autumn colors.

name: Lake Claiborne State Park
description: Known for its heavily wooded "hill country" landscape that offers immersive autumn colors in the northern part of the state.

name: Western Maryland (Garrett & Allegany Counties)
description: Famous for mountain landscapes; you can view the early-peaking colors via the Western Maryland Scenic Railroad or along the Great Allegheny Passage.

name: Sugarloaf Mountain
description: A popular central location offering a moderate hike with rewarding, panoramic summit views of the changing foliage.

name: Washington Monument State Park
description: Offers spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding autumn landscape from the historic monument tower.

name: The Berkshires (Western Massachusetts)
description: This region is arguably the state's premier leaf-peeping destination.

name: Mount Greylock State Reservation
description: As the state's highest point, it offers panoramic, sweeping views of the vibrant forest canopy.

name: Monument Mountain
description: A popular hike providing breathtaking vistas of the colorful landscape.

name: Route 2
description: A classic scenic highway traversing charming towns, offering excellent foliage views.

name: Fruitlands Museum (Harvard)
description: Scenic trails through meadows and woodlands that pop with autumn color.

name: Appleton Farms (Ipswich)
description: Offers extensive footpaths and farm roads perfect for an autumn stroll.

name: Houghton’s Pond (Blue Hills Reservation)
description: A great spot for a scenic hike surrounded by colors near the city.

name: Cape Ann (Rockport)
description: Known for "late peak" foliage, providing a beautiful coastal backdrop to the changing leaves.

name: Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park
description: Famous for vast, hilly forests and breathtaking views at Lake of the Clouds.

name: Keweenaw Peninsula
description: Offers some of the earliest and most dramatic foliage colors in the state.

name: Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore
description: A striking contrast of multicolored cliffs, blue waters, and autumn forests.

name: Tunnel of Trees
description: A legendary scenic drive offering an intimate experience under a changing canopy of leaves.

name: Sleeping Bear Dunes National Lakeshore
description: Provides a unique contrast between golden forests and massive sand dunes.

name: Jay Cooke State Park
description: Known for its iconic swinging bridge and beautiful river valley views framed by fall colors.

name: Maplewood State Park
description: Highly favored for its dense maple forests that turn brilliant shades of red and orange.

name: Itasca State Park
description: The headwaters of the Mississippi River feature massive pine and hardwood forests creating a classic autumn scene.

name: Hyland Hills Ski Area (Bloomington)
description: Offers fall chair lift rides for a panoramic view of the metro area's foliage.

name: Summit Avenue (St. Paul)
description: An urban fall experience featuring historic architecture and tree-lined streets.

name: Fort Snelling State Park
description: A convenient nature escape with lovely colors right in the heart of the cities.

name: Minneopa State Park (Mankato)
description: Known for its stunning waterfall and resident bison herd, which look incredible surrounded by fall foliage.

name: Natchez Trace Parkway
description: A relaxing, scenic drive with vibrant oaks, hickories, sweetgums, and maples.

name: Jeff Busby Park
description: Provides a great elevated overlook for leaf peeping.

name: Cypress Swamp
description: A unique, picturesque experience with its boardwalk and reflective waters under the changing leaves.

name: Tishomingo State Park
description: Features unique rock formations and dense Appalachian foothills hardwood forests, creating a stunning autumn backdrop.

name: Trace State Park
description: Noted for beautiful fall scenery and excellent hiking trails.

name: Mark Twain National Forest
description: Endless opportunities for viewing vibrant fall colors across rolling ridges and valleys.

name: Great River Road
description: Famous for its river valley vistas and forested bluffs stretching along the Mississippi River.

name: Taum Sauk Mountain State Park
description: The Mina Sauk Falls Trail provides excellent panoramic valley views.

name: Cuivre River State Park
description: Features winding roads and trails that heavily showcase the season's colors.

name: Castlewood State Park
description: Well-known for its bluff trails overlooking the Meramec River wrapped in seasonal color.

name: Hyalite Canyon
description: A stunning drive featuring golden trees following the creek.

name: Bridger Canyon
description: A beautiful drive known for its sweeping golden colors.

name: Bitterroot Valley
description: Offers a mix of forested terrain with a backdrop of the Bitterroot and Sapphire mountains in striking autumn hues.

name: Glacier National Park
description: Breathtaking alpine larch turning gold, contrasting with the rugged peaks and evergreen forests.

name: Pioneer Mountains Scenic Byway
description: A quiet route with golden willow-lined river bottoms and alpine meadows.

name: Southeast Montana (Medicine Rocks State Park)
description: Vast prairies and sandstone formations accented by cottonwoods turning bright yellow.

name: Indian Cave State Park (Shubert)
description: A premier spot featuring a mature hardwood forest that displays vibrant reds, oranges, and yellows.

name: Fontenelle Forest (Bellevue)
description: Over 1,400 acres of forest with excellent hiking and aerial courses to view the canopy from above.

name: Ponca State Park (Ponca)
description: Known for spectacular river bluffs and forested hills along the Missouri River.

name: Smith Falls State Park (Valentine)
description: Features wooded trails with sumac and quaking aspens leading to the state's highest waterfall.

name: Arbor Day Farm (Nebraska City)
description: A classic destination for autumn scenery and historic tree-lined beauty.

name: Brownville Area (Steamboat Trace Trail)
description: Beautiful waterfront views of the Missouri River framed by changing leaves.

name: Lamoille Canyon (Ruby Mountains)
description: Spectacular views of aspens cascading down mountainsides in brilliant shades of gold and red.

name: Great Basin National Park
description: Beautiful groves of aspen trees that brightly contrast with the rugged, high-altitude mountain peaks.

name: Mount Charleston (Spring Mountains)
description: A short drive from Vegas featuring lovely forests of aspen and oak for hiking and camping.

name: Angel Lake (near Wells)
description: Famous for dramatic fall displays where aspens create waves of color along the hillside.

name: Water Canyon Recreation Area
description: A great spot in Northern Nevada to immerse yourself in fall colors while hiking.

name: Floyd Lamb Park (Las Vegas)
description: A desert oasis featuring cottonwood and oak trees that turn vibrant yellow and orange.

name: The White Mountains
description: The premier region for fall colors in the state.

name: Kancamagus Highway (Route 112)
description: An iconic scenic drive offering some of the most famous foliage views in all of New England.

name: Franconia Notch State Park
description: Breathtaking mountain scenery, featuring vibrant spots like the Flume Gorge and Artist Bluff trail.

name: Crawford Notch State Park
description: Offers magnificent woodland vistas and scenic overlooks.

name: Mount Willard
description: Highly recommended for hikers looking for stunning panoramic views of the foliage.

name: Great North Woods
description: A fantastic, quieter option for spotting wildlife (like moose) completely surrounded by fall colors.

name: High Point State Park
description: The highest elevation in the state, offering early-season panoramic views of foliage across New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania.

name: Hacklebarney State Park
description: Features a dramatic contrast between colorful autumn leaves and green moss along the banks of the Black River.

name: Duke Farms (Hillsborough)
description: A sprawling estate with 18 miles of trails and woodlands that provide excellent opportunities for walking through the changing leaves.

name: Washington Rock State Park
description: Offers a famous 30-mile lookout over the Raritan Valley, providing a sweeping view of the fall colors.

name: Jenny Jump State Forest
description: A great spot for mountain views and tree-lined hiking trails showcasing vibrant seasonal colors.

name: Gila National Forest
description: Offers a more gradual unfolding of autumn colors, featuring a beautiful mix of reds, oranges, and yellows.

name: Sandia Mountains
description: Easily accessible via the Sandia Peak Aerial Tramway, offering quick access to high-altitude aspen groves and sweeping views.

name: Catskill Mountains
description: A premier destination known for its dramatic landscapes, waterfall backdrops, and charming towns like Woodstock.

name: Finger Lakes
description: Ideal for combining foliage with wine tasting. Watkins Glen State Park offers a stunning stone gorge and cascading waterfalls framed by autumn colors.

name: Hudson Valley
description: Offers a mix of historic towns, river views, and scenic state parks like Bear Mountain and Hudson Highlands.

name: Letchworth State Park
description: Often called the "Grand Canyon of the East," providing dramatic views of its gorge and waterfalls surrounded by colorful leaves.

name: New York City
description: Beautiful urban autumn scenery in Central Park, Prospect Park, and Fort Tryon Park.

name: Blue Ridge Parkway
description: Considered the "crown jewel" of fall foliage drives, with constant views and notable stops like the Linn Cove Viaduct and Graveyard Fields.

name: Grandfather Mountain
description: Famous for 360-degree views and the Mile High Swinging Bridge, providing a dramatic vantage point for the surrounding colorful mountains.

name: Great Smoky Mountains National Park
description: Offers vibrant, long-lasting displays due to its diverse elevations, with high-elevation areas peaking early.

name: Linville Gorge
description: Known as the "Grand Canyon of the East," featuring rugged trails and dramatic overlooks like Hawksbill Mountain for panoramic views.

name: Theodore Roosevelt National Park (Badlands)
description: Premier spots along the Little Missouri River where cottonwoods, aspens, and ash trees turn brilliant yellow.

name: Fort Abraham Lincoln State Park
description: Offers scenic views of colorful hillsides overlooking the Missouri River from trails like Little Soldier Loop.

name: Devils Lake
description: The woodlands surrounding this area provide a picturesque backdrop during the autumn season.

name: Turtle River State Park
description: Conveniently located near Grand Forks, offering a variety of trails through beautiful wooded areas.

name: Beaver Lake State Park
description: Excellent for relaxing under golden American elm trees.

name: Trenton Wildlife Management Area
description: Offers great wooded scenery around Trenton Lake near Williston.

name: Icelandic State Park
description: Contains over 200 acres of protected woodland perfect for leaf-peeping near the Gunlogson Nature Preserve.

name: Cuyahoga Valley National Park
description: Features the Ledges Overlook, the Cuyahoga Valley Scenic Railroad, and Brandywine Falls for unique foliage tours.

name: Hocking Hills State Park
description: Known for dramatic gorges and waterfalls where autumn foliage provides a vibrant backdrop to natural rock formations.

name: Mohican Memorial State Forest
description: Offers beautiful rolling hills and an iconic fire tower that provides a panoramic perspective of the changing canopy.

name: Holden Arboretum
description: Features diverse tree species and an Emergent Tower offering an elevated view of the forest.

name: Shawnee State Forest
description: Often referred to as the "Little Smokies," providing extensive trails and expansive forest views in the Appalachian foothills.

name: Ashtabula County
description: Famous for its scenic covered bridges that are incredibly picturesque when surrounded by autumn colors.

name: Osage Hills State Park
description: A hidden gem featuring beautiful drives and trails through oak, hickory, and sycamore trees.

name: Keystone Ancient Forest
description: Provides a unique experience with centuries-old cedars and towering post oaks showcasing rich, warm autumn tones.

name: Highway 10 (Gore to Tenkiller Lake)
description: A scenic drive combining a charming small-town atmosphere with vibrant autumn scenery along the water.

name: Columbia River Gorge
description: Famous for dramatic landscapes and iconic stops like Multnomah Falls, featuring a beautiful mix of maples and red alders.

name: Willamette Valley
description: Known for golden harvest days, historic covered bridges, and Silver Falls State Park where waterfalls are framed by brilliant autumn leaves.

name: Central Oregon
description: Areas like Skyliner Forest near Bend offer a striking contrast between towering ponderosa pines and golden quaking aspens.

name: Oregon Coast
description: Scenic drives along Highway 101 or the Three Capes Scenic Loop provide a unique coastal experience with misty forests turning shades of orange and yellow.

name: Pocono Mountains
description: Features extensive forests with three distinct color zones, allowing visitors to catch peak foliage multiple times across the region.

name: Allegheny National Forest
description: Offers vast, wooded landscapes perfect for those seeking a more remote and immersive leaf-peeping experience.

name: Laurel Highlands
description: Known for beautiful mountain scenery that typically sees peak colors around mid-October.

name: Lancaster County
description: Experience fall in Pennsylvania Dutch Country through covered bridge driving tours or scenic hot air balloon rides.

name: Cherry Springs State Park
description: The surrounding PA Wilds region is breathtaking during the fall, offering beautiful daytime scenery before the famous stargazing at night.

name: Pittsburgh (Frick Park)
description: Large urban parks provide excellent opportunities to see changing leaves without leaving the city limits.

name: Ocean Drive (Newport)
description: A 14-mile loop combining vibrant fall colors with stunning views of the Atlantic Ocean, historic mansions, and Fort Adams State Park.

name: Great Road (Lincoln)
description: Known for its colonial-era charm, passing historic farms and ponds with a dense display of fall colors.

name: Roger Williams Park (Providence)
description: A massive urban oasis with lakes, gardens, and diverse tree species turning brilliant shades of red, orange, and gold.

name: Colt State Park (Bristol)
description: Features a stunning route along the East Bay Bike Path with views of coves, marshes, and autumn trees.

name: Wolf Hill Forest Preserve (Smithfield)
description: A top-tier hiking area featuring a rocky ridge for a more rugged, forested foliage experience.

name: Caesars Head State Park
description: Known for its dramatic panoramic views of the Blue Ridge escarpment and the scenic hike to Raven Cliff Falls.

name: Table Rock State Park
description: A premier destination for hiking and viewing fall colors in the higher mountain elevations.

name: Jones Gap State Park
description: Offers an intimate and rugged experience with trails winding along the Middle Saluda River under a canopy of changing leaves.

name: Bald Rock Heritage Preserve
description: Offers stunning panoramic views of the colorful foothills accessible via a very short walk.

name: Paris Mountain State Park
description: A convenient option for excellent local foliage views near Greenville.

name: Custer State Park
description: Golden aspens create a spectacular backdrop for bison and other wildlife along the Wildlife Loop Road and Needles Highway.

name: Vanocker Canyon
description: A beautiful, less crowded drive between Sturgis and Nemo offering great leaf-peeping opportunities.

name: Newton Hills State Park
description: A dense forest of maple, oak, and sumac that is perfect for hiking or biking among the colors.

name: Great Smoky Mountains National Park (Cades Cove, Newfound Gap, Kuwohi)
description: A premier destination with high peaks and diverse forests providing sweeping views of changing colors.

name: Foothills Parkway
description: Known as the "Missing Link," offering unobstructed, panoramic views of the colorful mountains.

name: Cherohala Skyway
description: A National Scenic Byway providing high-elevation views and excellent photography opportunities.

name: Roan Mountain State Park
description: Offers a quieter leaf-peeping experience with vibrant mountain colors and beautiful cabins.

name: Natchez Trace Parkway
description: A historic and scenic drive perfect for a leisurely fall tour away from the Smokies' crowds.

name: Fall Creek Falls State Park
description: Famous for its stunning waterfalls set against a backdrop of brilliant autumn leaves.

name: Nashville Area (Radnor Lake, Percy Warner Park, Harpeth River)
description: Beautiful local leaf-peeping opportunities without leaving the city area.

name: Lost Maples State Natural Area (Vanderpool)
description: Famous for its bigtooth maples that turn brilliant shades of red, orange, and yellow.

name: Guadalupe Mountains National Park
description: High elevation areas like McKittrick Canyon offer some of the state's earliest and most beautiful foliage views.

name: Daingerfield State Park (Piney Woods)
description: Excellent for late-season color with a mix of sweetgums, oaks, and maples around the park's lake.

name: Garner State Park (Concan)
description: Beautiful views of cypress trees turning color along the Frio River.

name: Tyler State Park
description: A tranquil, heavily wooded atmosphere perfect for viewing Piney Woods fall foliage.

name: Caprock Canyons State Park
description: Dramatic scenery where yellow cottonwoods contrast beautifully with red canyon walls.

name: Guardsman Pass
description: A premier spot near Park City and Salt Lake City for viewing brilliant aspen groves.

name: Logan Canyon (US-89)
description: Known for deep, lush fall colors as it winds toward Bear Lake.

name: Scenic Byway 12
description: Offers diverse landscapes across varying elevations through Grand Staircase-Escalante and Dixie National Forest.

name: Big Cottonwood Canyon (Donut Falls, Dog Lake)
description: Popular trails offering excellent foliage views surrounded by mountains.

name: Bryce Canyon National Park
description: Offers a striking contrast between bright yellow aspens and red-orange hoodoos.

name: Stowe
description: The "fall foliage capital" of Vermont, offering breathtaking mountain views and quaint village charm.

name: Lake Willoughby (Northeast Kingdom)
description: Dramatic cliffs and deep blue water perfectly framed by rugged, less crowded autumn colors.

name: Woodstock
description: Famous for its classic village charm, historic covered bridges, and beautiful tree-lined streets.

name: Smugglers' Notch (Route 108)
description: A scenic mountain pass offering dramatic foliage drives through narrow, rock-walled passages.

name: Route 100
description: Vermont’s most famous scenic foliage route, paralleling the Green Mountains with continuous vistas.

name: Shenandoah National Park (Skyline Drive)
description: High-elevation famous spots like Stony Man Mountain and Old Rag Mountain offer spectacular leaf-peeping.

name: Blue Ridge Parkway
description: Known for iconic scenic overlooks and picturesque locations like Mabry Mill, perfect for autumn photography.

name: Grayson Highlands State Park
description: Famous for highland meadows, wild ponies, and sweeping panoramic mountain views.

name: North Cascades Highway (Lake Diablo Lookout, Washington Pass)
description: Premier scenic drive famous for golden alpine larches in the high country.

name: Washington Park Arboretum (Seattle)
description: Top urban destination for brilliant maples, especially along Azalea Way.

name: Olympic National Park (Hoh Rainforest, Hurricane Ridge)
description: Offers a mix of deep red vine maples and golden aspens.

name: Columbia River Gorge
description: Known for large groves of Big Leaf Maples and White Oaks.

name: New River Gorge National Park and Preserve
description: Iconic spot offering breathtaking views of the gorge and the famous bridge framed by autumn colors.

name: Blackwater Falls State Park
description: Picturesque waterfalls that look especially beautiful against the backdrop of changing fall leaves.

name: Highland Scenic Highway
description: High-elevation route offering expansive and dramatic mountain vistas for scenic drives.

name: Mercer County (Camp Creek, Pinnacle Rock)
description: Unique sandstone formations and great hiking surrounded by vibrant foliage.

name: Door County (Peninsula State Park, Cave Point)
description: Renowned for scenic bluffs, waterfront views, and charming towns surrounded by color.

name: Milwaukee Area (Seven Bridges Trail, Lake Park)
description: Beautiful woodland and lakeshore foliage close to the city.

name: Grand Teton National Park (Oxbow Bend, Moose-Wilson Road)
description: Iconic vistas where golden aspens, cottonwoods, and willows frame the Teton Range and reflect in the rivers.

name: Medicine Bow National Forest (Snowy Range)
description: High-elevation hiking with panoramic views of golden aspen and pine groves.
